返回本批次

十一岁末

The End of Eleventy

brennan.day 作者 ValentineC 分数 105 评论 48 2026/04/12 09:55
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 更新：由于电子邮件未发送，Kickstarter 已被取消并重新安排了几个月的时间，尽管在一天之内就达到了目标，但破坏了该项目的“势头”。
  2. 昨天，Font Awesome 团队为一个名为 Build Awesome 和 Build Awesome Pro 的新项目启动了 Kickstarter，希望筹集 40,000 美元。它已经达到了该融资目标。什么是构建卓越？简单来说，它是 11ty/Eleventy 的品牌重塑。或者更确切地说，这是十一的结束。我在这件事上有个人利害关系。 11ty 是我的网站以及其他数千个网站的构建和支持平台。我支持 Open Collective 上的 11ty 并为该框架创建了主题。那么我对此有何感想呢？但在我解释为什么我（和许多其他 11ty 开发者）不庆祝这个 Kickstarter 的巨大成功之前，让我们首先回答这个问题：11ty 到底是什么？嗯，它是一个静态站点生成器。好吧，但是静态站点生成器到底是什么？为什么它对互联网的未来如此重要？我很高兴你问了。第一部分：非动态静态网站的简史早于动态内容管理系统及其精美的后端和数据库。在互联网的早期，所有网站都只是静态 HTML 文件的集合。动态站点始于通用网关接口 (CGI) 的出现，后来出现了 PHP、ASP 和 Ruby on Rails 等服务器端脚本语言，以及 WordPress 等数据库驱动的 CMS 框架，后者为整个互联网的约 43% 提供支持。值得庆幸的是，随着现代静态发电机的兴起，钟摆开始转向静态方法。更安全、更简单的托管，而且速度更快。本质上，您所需要做的就是构建一个包含一些模板语言和 Markdown 文件的文件夹，最终您将获得一个完全渲染的网站。这是时间表：
  3. Jekyll (2008) 由 GitHub 联合创始人 Tom Preston-Werner 创建，被称为“黑客博客”并重新流行了 SSG，特别是它集成到 GitHub Pages 中，这意味着 GH 上的任何开发人员都可以使用该框架立即在 username.github.io 上创建一个网站。五年后，用 Go 编写的 Hugo (2013) 因其更快的构建速度而受到关注，使其适合大规模静态站点，这与 Ruby-on-Rails 依赖的 Jekyll 不同。 Gatsby（2015）是一个基于 React 的 SSG，引入了“内容网格”并利用 GraphQL 进行数据源，旨在提供现代、高性能的 Web 开发体验。 Eleventy (2017) 最后被定位为“反框架”SSG，为更固执己见的工具提供轻量级、灵活的替代方案。
  4. 第二部分：11ty：起源 Eleventy 是由 Zach Leatherman 创作的，直接从 Jekyll 中汲取灵感。但他想要一种替代方案，既可以利用新兴的 Node.js 生态系统，又不需要强加严格的客户端 JavaScript 框架。 11ty 在三件事上做得很好：灵活性、利用 JavaScript 以及避免成为 JavaScript 框架。它支持多个模板引擎，允许 Web 开发人员轻松迁移、混合搭配。 Liquid、Nunjucks、Markdown、Handlebars 和 EJS 都在一个项目中。虽然 Eleventy 可以使用庞大的 npm 生态系统来进行构建过程，但它故意避免指定客户端 JavaScript。谁用11ty？ NASA、CERN、TC39 委员会、W3C、Google、Microsoft、Mozilla、Apache、freeCodeCamp 等等。 A11y 项目随 Eleventy 1.0 一起启动，其首席开发人员 Eric Bailey 指出，近三年后，该网站仍然可以安装并从冷启动运行，没有任何并发​​症。 Leatherman 最初受 Netlify 聘用，在 Eleventy 全职工作，但在 2024 年 9 月，11ty 转到 Font Awesome，Leatherman 也加入了他们的团队。现在，到了 2026 年，Eleventy 正在“Build Awesome”，定位为 Font Awesome 和 Web Awesome 的一体化网站构建器。但为什么？我写这篇文章是因为 Build Awesome 试图回答一个我多年来亲眼所见困扰 Web 开发领域的问题：第三部分：……我们他妈的如何从中赚钱？到 2015 年，一个术语被编入法典：Jamstack（JavaScript、API 和 Markup）。这一概念由 Netlify 首席执行官 Matt Biilmann 大力推广，他认为将前端与后端分离、在构建时预渲染静态 HTML 并通过 API 连接到服务，是现代 Web 的正确架构。默认情况下，它快速、安全且可扩展。 Jamstack 框架打开了一个商业机会。如果静态网站是未来，谁将构建工具、托管和工作流程来大规模支持它们？盖茨比成为风险投资创业界的宠儿。它承诺提供一个 GraphQL 数据层，可以在构建时从任何 CMS 或 API 中提取数据。到 2019 年，Gatsby 已经筹集了 1500 万美元的 A 轮融资；到 2020 年，B 轮融资金额高达 2800 万美元。来自 Vercel 的 Next.js 作为一个全栈 React 框架出现，模糊了静态和服务器渲染之间的界限，直接与 Gatsby 竞争。该公司的估值达到 93 亿美元，部分归功于这个框架（但主要是人工智能）。市场上充斥着资金充足、市场良好的选择。 Gatsby Inc. 筹集了总计超过 4600 万美元的风险投资，试图通过提供专业托管和内容管理功能的“Gatsby Cloud”平台获利。尽管投入巨额资金，Gatsby Cloud 未能获得硅谷“曲棍球棒”般的增长，最终于 2023 年 2 月被 Netlify 收购。收购后，Netlify 宣布关闭 Gatsby Cloud，截至撰写本文时，Gatsby 本身已死，不再维护。还有 Stackbit，它的目标是成为各种 SSG 的“站点构建器”，承诺简化开发工作流程。然而，支持多种 SSG 和无头 CMS 组合的复杂性实际上是不可能的。 Stackbit 随后将其重点转向为无头 CMS 提供“可视化编辑”层，允许内容编辑者无需直接代码交互即可实时查看更改。也许并不令人意外的是，它们也被 Netlify 收购，然后变成了 Netlify Create，然后悄然消失。并谈论魔鬼！ Netlify 和 Vercel 等公司围绕 JAMstack 应用程序的托管和部署服务建立了业务。他们的策略是支持流行的开源 SSG（例如，Netlify 对 Eleventy 的支持和 Vercel 对 Next.js 的支持）作为“亏损领导者”，以吸引用户使用其付费托管和基础设施平台。你看到问题了，对吧？这种模式将基础设施而不是 SSG 本身货币化，并且开源项目仍然依赖于这些较大平台的商誉和战略联盟。第四部分：Leatherman 的开源 Dread Leatherman 作为 Eleventy 的创建者和 BDFL 首席维护者，一直是可持续开源发展的积极倡导者。他最近发布了一个令人大开眼界的播客节目，题为“Eleventy 如何生存：资金、增长和开源现实”。莱瑟曼谈到了在资源有限和个人牺牲巨大的情况下维护一个被广泛采用和关键基础设施的项目所固有的斗争。维护者面临着倦怠，需要设定界限才能真正实现可持续发展；曲棍球棒式增长的风投心态与开源生态系统是对立的。 Leatherman 加入 Font Awesome 是因为他相信该公司与他一样致力于“无聊”（可靠且稳定）技术和可持续发展，这一点很明显他一边录制这个播客，一边积极规划充满希望的赚钱项目“Build Awesome”。第五部分：我们以前看过这部电影至此，我们就到了这里。 Font Awesome 决定尝试通过将静态站点生成器重新命名为笨重的全栈 CMS 的可访问替代品来货币化。看看专业版的功能：
  5. 协作可视化编辑（“无头 CMS”的另一种说法）内置浏览器（无需本地开发设置，无需终端）高级内置模板和托管导入工具

Original Summary

  1. UPDATE: The Kickstarter has been cancelled and rescheduled for a few months from now due to emails not being sent, ruining the project's "momentum" despite reaching their goal in a single day.
  2. Yesterday, the Font Awesome team launched a Kickstarter for a new project called Build Awesome and Build Awesome Pro, looking to raise $40,000 USD. And it has already reached that funding goal. What is Build Awesome? Simply put, it's a rebrand of 11ty/Eleventy. Or rather, it is the end of Eleventy. I have personal stakes in this. 11ty is what my site, and thousands of others, are built and powered with. I support 11ty on Open Collective and have created themes for the framework. So how do I feel about this? But before I get into why I (and many other 11ty devs) are not celebrating this hugely successful Kickstarter, let's first answer the question: What the hell is 11ty? Well, it's a static site generator. Okay, but what the hell is a static site generator, and why does it matter for the literal future of the Internet so much? I'm so glad you asked. Part One: A Brief History of the Non-Dynamic Static websites predate dynamic content management systems with their fancy backends and databases. In the early days of the Internet, all websites were mere collections of static HTML files. Dynamic sites started with the advent of the Common Gateway Interface (CGI) and later server-side scripting languages like PHP, ASP, and Ruby on Rails, along with database-driven CMS frameworks such as WordPress, which powers roughly 43% of the entire Internet. Thankfully, the pendulum began to swing back towards static approaches with the rise of modern static-site generators. More secure, simpler hosting, and so much faster. Essentially, all you need to do is build a folder with some template languages and Markdown files and you end up with a fully-rendered website. Here's the timeline:
  3. Jekyll (2008) was created by GitHub co-founder Tom Preston-Werner, dubbed "blogging for hackers" and repopularized SSGs, particularly with its integration into GitHub Pages, meaning any dev on GH could make a website instantly at username.github.io with the framework. Hugo (2013), five years later and written in Go, gained traction for its much faster build speed, making it suitable for large-scale static sites unlike the Ruby-on-Rails dependent Jekyll. Gatsby (2015) was a React-based SSG introducing the "content mesh" and leveraged GraphQL for data sourcing, aiming to be a modern, performant web development experience. Eleventy (2017), finally, positioned as an "anti-framework" SSG, offering a lightweight, flexible alternative to more opinionated tools.
  4. Part Two: 11ty: Origins Eleventy was created by Zach Leatherman, drawing direct inspiration from Jekyll. But he wanted an alternative that leveraged the burgeoning Node.js ecosystem without imposing a rigid client-side JavaScript framework. 11ty does three things well: flexibility, leveraging JavaScript, and avoiding being a JavaScript framework. It supports multiple templating engines, allowing webdevs to migrate easily, and mix and match. Liquid, Nunjucks, Markdown, Handlebars, and EJS all within a single project. While Eleventy can use the vast npm ecosystem for the build process, it deliberately avoids dictating client-side JavaScript. Who uses 11ty? NASA, CERN, the TC39 committee, W3C, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, Apache, freeCodeCamp, to name a few. The A11y Project launched with Eleventy 1.0 and its lead developer Eric Bailey noted that nearly three years later, the site could still install and run from a cold start with no complications. Leatherman was initially hired by Netlify to work on Eleventy full-time, but in September 2024, 11ty moved to Font Awesome, with Leatherman joining their team. Now, in 2026, Eleventy is "Build Awesome", angled as the all-in-one site builder for Font Awesome and Web Awesome. But why? I'm writing this because Build Awesome is trying to answer a question that I've seen first-hand plague this space of web development for years: Part Three: ...How the fuck do we make money off of this? By 2015, a term was being codified: the Jamstack (JavaScript, APIs, and Markup). The concept, popularized heavily by Netlify CEO Matt Biilmann, argued that decoupling the frontend from the backend, pre-rendering static HTML at build time and connecting to services via APIs, was the correct architecture for the modern web. It was fast, secure, and scalable by default. The Jamstack framing opened a commercial opportunity. If static sites were the future, who would build the tools, the hosting, and the workflows to support them at scale? Gatsby became the darling of the VC-funded startup world. It promised a GraphQL data layer that could pull from any CMS or API at build time. By 2019, Gatsby had raised $15 million in Series A funding; by 2020, a $28 million Series B followed. Next.js, from Vercel, emerged as a full-stack React framework that blurred the line between static and server-rendered, competing directly with Gatsby. The company reached a valuation of $9.3 billion in part due to this framework (but mostly AI). The market was crowded with well-funded, well-marketed options. Gatsby Inc. raised over a total $46 million in venture capital, attempting to monetize through the "Gatsby Cloud" platform, offering specialized hosting and content management features. Despite huge investment, Gatsby Cloud failed to get the Silicon Valley "hockey-stick" growth and was ultimately acquired by Netlify in February 2023. Following the acquisition, Netlify announced the shutdown of Gatsby Cloud, and as of writing, Gatsby itself is dead and no longer maintained. There was also Stackbit, which aimed to be a "site builder" for various SSGs, promising to simplify the development workflow. However, the complexity of supporting a multitude of SSG and headless CMS combinations was actually impossible. Stackbit subsequently pivoted its focus to providing a "Visual Editing" layer for headless CMS, allowing content editors to see changes in real-time without direct code interaction. Perhaps unsurprisingly, they too were acquired by Netlify and then turned into Netlify Create before quietly being sunset altogether. And speak of the Devil! Companies like Netlify and Vercel have built businesses around hosting and deployment services for JAMstack applications. Their strategy is to support popular open-source SSGs (e.g., Netlify's support for Eleventy and Vercel's backing of Next.js) as "loss leaders" to attract users to their paid hosting and infrastructure platforms. You see the problem, right? This model monetizes the infrastructure rather than the SSG itself, and the open-source projects remain dependent on the goodwill and strategic alignment of these larger platforms. Part Four: Leatherman's Open Source Dread Leatherman, as the creator and BDFL lead maintainer of Eleventy, has been a vocal advocate for sustainable open-source development. He recently released an eye-opening podcast episode titled "How Eleventy Survived: Funding, Growth, and Open Source Reality". Leatherman spoke of the inherent struggle of maintaining a project that becomes too-widely adopted and critical infrastructure, with limited resources and significant personal sacrifice. Maintainers face burnout and boundaries need to be put in place for any of this to actually be sustainable; the VC mindset of hockey-stick growth was antithetical to the open source ecosystem. Leatherman joined Font Awesome because he believed the company shared his commitment to "boring" (reliable and stable) technology and sustainable development, and it's clear that he recorded this podcast while actively planning the hopeful money-maker, Build Awesome. Part Five: We've Seen This Movie Before With that, we've ended up exactly here. With Font Awesome deciding to attempt to monetize the static-site generator by rebranding it as an accessible alternative to clunky full-stack CMSes. Just take a look at the pro features:
  5. Collaborative visual editing (another way to say "headless CMS") Build-in-a-browser (no local dev setup, no terminal needed) Premium built-in templates and hosted import tools

评论（默认中文）

charles_f 2026/04/12 11:21
我已经使用相同版本的 Jekyll，使用相同过时的、已停产的 ruby​​ 版本，已经超过 10 年了。我 拒绝 学习有关 ruby​​ 的任何知识，或者花时间升级 Jekyll 或我使用的任何两个插件，我对此感到一种奇怪的自豪。它有效，它生成我的博客，我不想让它做任何其他事情。我不知道它是如何工作的了。据我所知，杰基尔已经被抛弃了。那个版本的 ruby​​ 可能充满了错误和安全漏洞，我为什么要关心呢？它仅在我生成网站时使用，在一个不与任何东西通信的 Docker 容器中。

Eleventy 可能不会收到新功能，您的网站仍然可以运行。

I've been using the same version of Jekyll, using the same outdated, discontinued version of ruby, for more than 10y. I refuse to learn anything about ruby, or spend any time upgrading Jekyll or any of the 2 plug-ins I use, and I take a weird pride in that. It works, it generates my blog, I don't want it to do anything else. I have no idea how it works anymore. For all I know Jekyll has been abandoned. That version of ruby might be riddled with bugs and security holes, and why would I care? it's only used when I generate the website, in a docker container that doesn't talk to anything.

Eleventy might not receive new features, your website will still work.

在 HN 中查看
mooreds 2026/04/12 11:29
> Eleventy 可能不会收到新功能，您的网站仍然可以运行。

伙计们，用一句话来概括 SSG 的魅力。

我也不知道 HTML 中有任何 CVE。

> Eleventy might not receive new features, your website will still work.

The beauty of SSGs, in one sentence, folks.

I'm not aware of any CVEs in HTML, either.

在 HN 中查看
[deleted] 2026/04/12 13:52 deleted

[已删除]

在 HN 中查看
PaulHoule 2026/04/12 10:56
SSG 的特点是，您只需要它们提供的功能的一小部分以及用途：因此，您可以在 HTML 中记住一些简单的链接语法，而不是它们

  <a href="there">description</a>
有一些奇怪和不规则的东西我总是必须在 Markdown 和各种其他 Markdown WTF 的手册中查找。 每次我尝试开始使用 SSG 建立个人网站时，我都会因为看到数百个丑陋的主题而感到沮丧，对神秘而蹩脚的云端构建系统感到沮丧，对定制它们的前景感到沮丧等等。所以我开始尝试，永远不会完成，六个月后回来进行另一次失败的尝试。

当我真的需要一个看起来像从 UFO 上掉下来的登陆页面时，我在 Vite-React 中做到了（使用语义组件真是一种乐趣，比如 write

   <Event date="2026-04-18">Earth Day Parade Ithaca Commons</Event>
它是一个简单的 python 脚本，用于将 dist 文件上传到 S3（没有“github 操作出了什么问题”），使 Cloudfront [1] 无效，提取元数据，维护元数据数据库。 有一条清晰的路径可以扩展系统，以准确地完成我将来想做的事情，这与某些 SSG 不同，当我想要做出重大改变时，我必须在六个月内从头开始重新学习……并在周末将其启动并运行并呈现在最终用户面前。

也就是说，SSG 没有商业潜力，因为任何有能力维护和定制 SSG 的个人或组织都可以从头开始创建一个能够以更少的成本和精力完全满足他们需要的服务，并且只有通过催眠人们以其他方式思考才能成功——在许多软件领域，这种情况每天都会发生，但我认为 SSG 不会，就像那些人们会在睡觉时梦想着 Drupal 和 Wordpress 一样。

[1] ...如果我想转移到一些类似的平台，我只需实现它，而不是与“插件”和“模块”以及其他过于复杂的扩展机制作斗争

The thing about SSGs is that you only need a small percentage of the functionality they offer and for what: so instead of some simple syntax for links you can remember in HTML

  <a href="there">description</a>
there is something weird and irregular I always have to look up in the manual in Markdown and all sorts of other Markdown WTFs. Every time I tried to get started on a personal site with an SSG I would get depressed looking at hundreds of ugly themes, get depressed with the mysterious and crappy cloud-side build systems, get depressed with the prospect of customizing them, etc. So I'd start experimenting, never finish and come back six months to make another attempt that fails.

When I really needed a landing page that looked like it fell off a UFO I did it in Vite-React (such a joy to use semantic components, like write

   <Event date="2026-04-18">Earth Day Parade Ithaca Commons</Event>
and it is a simple python script that uploads the dist files to S3 (no "WTF went wrong with the github action") invalidates Cloudfront [1], extracts metadata, maintains the metadata database. There's a clear path to extending the system to do exactly what I want to do in the future unlike some SSG which I will have to relearn from scratch in six months when I want to make a big change... and had it up and running and in front of end users in a weekend.

That is, SSG has no commercial potential because any individual or organization which is capable of maintaining and customizing an SSG can create one from scratch that does exactly what they need with less cost and effort and success is only possible through hypnotizing people into thinking otherwise -- in many fields of software this happens every day but I think not SSG, like those people are going to stay asleep and dream of Drupal and Wordpress.

[1] ... and if I want to move to some similar platform I just implement it instead of struggle with "plugins" and "modules" and other overcomplicated extension mechanisms

在 HN 中查看
Tomte 2026/04/12 11:18
https://soupault.net/ 是关于使用纯 HTML，但从中创建索引页、RSS 提要等。您甚至可以不用 frontmatter，因为类似 CSS 的选择器允许这些元页面从 HTML 页面检索标题、日期等。
https://soupault.net/ is about using plain HTML, but doing index pages, RSS feeds and so on from that. You even get away with not having frontmatter, because CSS like selectors allow those meta pages to retrieve title, date etc. from the HTML pages.
在 HN 中查看
girvo 2026/04/12 12:25
哈，当然是用 Ocaml 写的！

作为学习练习，我用 ocaml 编写了自己的小 SSG，我忘记了它是一种多么好的语言。工具仍然有点粗糙，但比以前好多了

我的小博客（在我的个人资料中）是使用它构建的： https://github.com/girvo/jgirvin_blog_ocaml

我确信 Ocaml 很糟糕，但在 YOCaml 和 Soupault 之间，最好的 SSG 都是用这种语言编写的。确实令人着迷

Hah, of course it’s written in Ocaml!

As a learning exercise, I wrote my own little SSG in ocaml, and man I forgot how nice a language it is. Tooling is still a bit rough but lots better than it used to be

My little blog (in my profile) is built using it: https://github.com/girvo/jgirvin_blog_ocaml

Horrible Ocaml I’m sure, but between YOCaml and Soupault, the best SSGs are all written in this language. Fascinating really

在 HN 中查看
_virtu 2026/04/12 13:46
为什么 JavaScript 生态系统以没有框架而自豪？这就是让我离开生态系统的原因。 JavaScript 是我关注的第一门专业语言，作为一名年轻工程师，我喜欢以成长为导向的心态，但令我恼火的是我必须不断地用不同的工具重建同一组模式，这就是 JS 生态系统的特殊选择你自己的冒险地狱。

我把它留给了长生不老药和凤凰，再也没有回头。在 JS 生态系统中，没有真正的所有权和方向可以与 Jose Valim 和 Chris McCord 相媲美。它是如此碎片化，以至于失去了维护 JS 代码库的乐趣。

Why does the JavaScript ecosystem pride itself in not having a framework? That’s the very thing that drove me out of the ecosystem. JavaScript was my first professional language of focus and I was in love with the growth oriented mentality as a younger engineer, but the part that irked me was that I had to constantly be rebuilding the same set of patterns with different tooling, which is the special choose your own adventure hell that is the JS ecosystem.

I left it for elixir and Phoenix and never looked back. There’s just no true ownership and direction that can come close to that of Jose Valim and Chris McCord in the JS ecosystem. It’s so fragmented that it takes the fun out of maintaining a JS codebase.

在 HN 中查看
preommr 2026/04/12 11:05
更喜欢astro。

这有点违反直觉，但增加的复杂性会导致更简单的项目，更容易长期维护。我有简单的降价文件，以及一个适合我的单独的、基于代码的转换过程。

另外，Eleven 的文档总是让我感到困惑。我几乎得到了“这很简单，我们不需要解释”的印象。而 astro 的文档更容易访问；在少数情况下，我想做一些事情，而 astro 有一个确切的例子。我不必进行猜测，只需按照创建者的意图遵循示例即可。诸如此类的事情很重要。

Much prefer astro.

It's somewhat counterintuitive, but the added complexity leads to simpler projects that are easier to maintain long term. I have simple markdown files, and a separate, code-based conversion process that works well for me.

Also the documentation for eleventy was always confusing to me. I almost got the impression that "it's so simple, we don't have to explain it". Whereas astro's documentation is much more accesible; there were a handful of cases where there was something I wanted to do and astro had an example of exactly that. I didn't have to do guesswork, just follow the examples in the way the creators intended. Stuff like that is important.

在 HN 中查看
adzm 2026/04/12 12:51
Astro 很棒，并且只需查看代码和现有扩展即可轻松扩展。强烈推荐它。拥有实际反应材料的岛屿也非常有用。
Astro is great, and easily extensible just by looking at the code and existing extensions too. Highly recommend it. Having the islands of actual react stuff is incredibly useful as well.
在 HN 中查看
xp84 2026/04/12 10:44
令人惊讶的是，SSG 与 Wordpress 仍然是一场战斗……我真的对网络上使用 Wordpress 的网站数量感到震惊，为每个页面视图使用 PHP 动态组装标记，冒着不断被黑客攻击等风险，当它们总共有 7 或 100 个页面时，即使在垃圾笔记本电脑或小型 ec2 实例上，这些页面也可以在大约 8 秒内预渲染为 HTML 文件。真的没关系。

对于那些在这些网站上定期发布更新的人来说，有一些很棒且便宜的 WP 插件可以将整个网站作为静态导出到 FTP 或 S3 等，因此您可以在 IP 限制后面对实际的 WP 进行防火墙，并从 S3/其他方式托管实际的面向公众的网站。

SSGs versus Wordpress is surprisingly still a battle… I’m genuinely shocked at the number of sites on the Net that use Wordpress, dynamically assembling markup with PHP for every page view, risking constant hacking and stuff, when they have a total of like 7 or 100 pages, which could all be pre-rendered to HTML files in roughly 8 seconds on even a junky laptop or X-small ec2 instance. It really is okay.

For those who post regular updates on those sites, there are great and cheap WP plugins that export the whole site as static to something like FTP or S3, so you can just firewall the actual WP behind an IP restriction and host the actual public-facing site from S3/whatever.

在 HN 中查看
yallpendantools 2026/04/12 12:57
营销部门是否有一个可行的工作流程来通过 Wordpress 使用 SSG？

- 所见即所得编辑器是赌注。当我在一台没有反应的 Macbook 上进行 `ps -eaf` 编辑时，营销部门可爱的人们曾经以为我在进行黑客攻击。

- 他们在帖子中“放置”图像。他们不会“上传图像并使用 CSS 定位它”。

- 这是营销部门，所以他们 有 拥有各种花里胡哨的东西。至少是跟踪，顶多是一些不起眼的集成插件，作为一名工程师，我对此没有什么好话。社交整合和“您可能也喜欢......”部分也浮现在脑海中。

> 廉价的 WP 插件，将整个站点静态导出到 FTP 或 S3 等，这样您就可以在 IP 限制后面对实际的 WP 进行防火墙，并从 S3/其他方式托管实际的面向公众的站点。

并不是说我有丰富的 WP 经验，但除非你能给我命名一个在狂野西部具有良好街头信誉的实际插件，否则我会说这并不像你听起来那么容易。对于其中一个，您刚刚描述了一个非常基本的数据管道，即使很少有人也必须支持和维护它。另外，从经验来看，插件并不总是能够与其他插件很好地配合。我曾经尝试从 WP 中导出我非常基本的个人网站，结果发现脚注全乱了（我现在不知道，但当时我用插件处理脚注）。

Is there a tenable workflow for the marketing department to use a SSG over Wordpress?

- WYSIWYG editor is table stakes. The lovely folks at marketing once thought I was hacking when I `ps -eaf`-ed in an unresponsive Macbook.

- They "put" images in their post. They don't "upload the image and position it with CSS".

- It's the marketing department so they have to have all sorts of bells and whistles. At the very least tracking, at most some obscure integration plug-in that as an engineer I have no kind words for. Social integrations and "You may also like..." sections also come to mind.

> cheap WP plugins that export the whole site as static to something like FTP or S3, so you can just firewall the actual WP behind an IP restriction and host the actual public-facing site from S3/whatever.

Not that I have extensive WP experience but unless you can name me an actual plugin that has good street cred for being used in the wild wild west, I'm gonna say this is not as easy as you make it sound. For one you just described a very rudimentary data pipeline which someone has to support and maintain even infrequently. Also, speaking from experience, plugins don't always play nice with other plugins. I once tried to export my very basic personal site out of WP to find the footnotes all messed up (I don't know now but back then I handled footnotes with a plugin).

在 HN 中查看
evanelias 2026/04/12 11:57
讽刺的是，WordPress 流行的最初主要原因之一是它的动态特性。它取代了 Movable Type，后者是一个极其强大且可扩展的静态站点生成器。

对我来说，这篇文章的时间线没有提及 Movable Type，这让我很疯狂，并且在某一时刻它链接到另一位作者题为“静态的完整历史：WordPress Headless 的开始”的文章，其中 还 完全没有提及活字印刷。现在我感觉自己老了:/

Ironically, one of the original major reasons WordPress became popular was its dynamic nature. It dethroned Movable Type, which was an extremely powerful and extensible static site generator.

It's wild to me that this post's timeline makes no mention whatsoever of Movable Type, and at one point it links to another author's post titled "A Complete History of Static: The Beginning to WordPress Headless" which also makes no mention whatsoever of Movable Type. Now I feel old :/

在 HN 中查看
teekert 2026/04/12 13:20
我在 Hugo 上启动了客户的网站，在 2 天内，我每 30 分钟就为他们编辑一些内容（有点夸张）。他们想要一些可以编辑的东西，他们不做 Markdown，他们不手动编写他们想要将图像拖到帖子和页面中的 URL。

那么，再见了，雨果。

I started my client's site on Hugo, withing 2 days I was editing something for them every 30 mins (slight exaggeration). They wanted something they could edit, they don't do Markdown, they don't manually write URLs they want to drag images into their posts and pages.

So bye bye Hugo.

在 HN 中查看
CJefferson 2026/04/12 12:54
对我来说，wordpress 安装提供的是 GUI。我帮助一些人安装了 WordPress，最后我设置了一个私人 WordPress 安装，然后我运行一个静态镜像网站的脚本——这有点黑客，我确信可以做得更好，但只要我隐藏私人 WordPress 安装，这意味着我不需要担心保持它是最新的。

我还没有找到一个静态生成器，它具有像 wordpress 一样好的所见即所得界面。

To me, the thing wordpress installs offer is the GUI. I help a few people with wordpress installs, and I've ended up setting up a private wordpress install, and then I run a script which mirrors the website statically -- this is moderately hacky, and I'm sure could be done better, but as long as I hide the private wordpress install, it means I don't need to worry about keeping it up to date.

I haven't found a static generator which has as nice a WYSIWYG interface as wordpress.

在 HN 中查看
nabeards 2026/04/12 13:26
对于那些寻求所见即所得 SSG 的人，请查看 Publii。除了作为用户之外，我没有任何关系，但我希望更多的人意识到这一点。
For those looking for an SSG with WYSIWYG, please take a look at Publii. I have no affiliation other than being a user, but I wish more people were aware of it.
在 HN 中查看
faangguyindia 2026/04/12 12:15
我最近将我的所有网站从 WordPress 上移走了。 在过去的几年里，其中一些也通过插件漏洞被黑客攻击。之后我不得不使用“安全”插件，我没有任何问题，但仍然......

我爬行了自己的网站并下载了每个网站，然后转换为 markdown，然后使用静态网站生成器（在 javascript 中自定义）

免费在 cloudflare 页面上运行，没有停机时间或“费用”。

如果你想看到结果： https://aretecodex.pages.dev/guides/recomposition

有几个问题：

要编辑内容，我必须使用“图像粘贴”插件并在 .vscode 中的项目设置中配置其基本目录、图像路径

我失去了评论/点赞功能。

我失去了“搜索”

I recently moved all my website from WordPress. In last few year couple of them had got hacked via plugin exploits as well. I had to use "security" plugins after that i didn't have any issue but still...

I crawled own website and downloaded each, and converted to markdown then used static site generator (custom in javascript)

runs on cloudflare pages for free with no downtimes or "fee".

if you want to see result: https://aretecodex.pages.dev/guides/recomposition

Couple of problems:

To edit content i've to use "image paste" plugin and configure its base directory, image path in project setting in .vscode

I lost the comment/upvote feature.

I lost "search"

在 HN 中查看
mooreds 2026/04/12 11:30
WordPress 有：

* 安排帖子的能力

*大量插件

* 很多人知道如何使用它

* 合理的所见即所得界面

据我所知，大多数 SSG 都在其中一个或多个维度上失败了。

Wordpress has:

* the ability to schedule posts

* a ton of plugins

* a lot of people who know how to use it

* a reasonable WYSIWYG interface

As far as I know, most SSGs fall down on one or more of those dimensions.

在 HN 中查看
corny 2026/04/12 13:36
* 实时预览
* live preview
在 HN 中查看
joemaller1 2026/04/12 12:09
第三颗子弹。我在工作中使用了大量的 WordPress。客户要求它，因为他们知道如何使用它。
3rd bullet. I do a ton of WordPress at work. Clients asked for it because they know how to use it.
在 HN 中查看
nchmy 2026/04/12 12:57
你听说过缓存吗？因为它可以做与您刚才描述的完全相同的事情，但 WP 也可以是动态的，具有可视页面和帖子生成器等......
have you ever heard of caching? because it can do the exact same thing as what you just described, yet WP can also be dynamic, have visual page and post builders, etc...
在 HN 中查看
busterarm 2026/04/12 12:32
如果您基本有能力的话，WordPress 黑客/插件安全问题已经解决了 10 多年。 特别是如果您使用 WP Engine 或 Pantheon 之类的东西进行托管。
The WordPress hacking/plugin security issue has been a solved problem for well over 10 years now if you're even basically competent. Especially if you're using something like WP Engine or Pantheon for hosting.
在 HN 中查看
nchmy 2026/04/12 13:01
这些主机在插件安全性方面有何独特之处？
what is unique about those hosts with regards to plugin security?
在 HN 中查看
prepend 2026/04/12 11:04
可悲的是，SSG 无法赚钱。它们也不应该，因为它们很简单，而且要点就是简单，不需要复杂的资源来构建或托管。

我祝他们一切顺利。

Sadly, SSGs can’t make money. Nor should they, because they are simple and sort of the whole point is to be simple and not require complicated resources to build or host.

I wish them the best.

在 HN 中查看
PaulHoule 2026/04/12 11:07
例如您无法从头开始构建自己的 Drupal，但是 你 有能力从头开始构建 SSG，甚至可能比从头开始定制现有 SSG 并躲避 WTF 更简单。
e.g. you can't afford to build your own Drupal from scratch but you can afford to build an SSG from scratch and it may even be simpler than customizing an existing SSG from scratch and dodging the WTF.
在 HN 中查看
replygirl 2026/04/12 13:49
如果有人想知道，这张照片是科隆的旧道依茨工厂
if anyone is wondering, the photo is of the old deutz works in cologne
在 HN 中查看
trendbuilder 2026/04/12 10:59
Eleventy 的优势始终在于其简单性和对开发人员选择的尊重，但单独维护一个开源项目确实很残酷。具有讽刺意味的是，它帮助普及的 JAMstack 浪潮最终产生了资金雄厚、能够负担得起全职团队的竞争对手。希望扎克能找到好的地方——他的工作塑造了我们很多人对静态网站架构的看法。
Eleventy's strength was always its simplicity and respect for the developer's choices, but maintaining an open source project solo is genuinely brutal. The irony is that the JAMstack wave it helped popularize eventually produced well-funded competitors that could afford full-time teams. Hope Zach lands somewhere good — his work shaped how a lot of us think about static site architecture.
在 HN 中查看
gnabgib 2026/04/12 10:23
38 天前由 OP (5 分) https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47247541

跟进（OP） 取消 （5分，1个月前） https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47282675

有关的 简介：打造精彩 （3分） https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47245750

38d ago by OP (5 points) https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47247541

Follow up (by OP) Cancelled (5 points, 1 month ago) https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47282675

Related Introducing: Build Awesome (3 points) https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47245750

在 HN 中查看
InvisibleUp 2026/04/12 13:45
> 事实是，静态站点生成器还没有成功的 CMS，因为唯一关心创建静态站点的人更喜欢使用（免费且本地的）IDE 和终端。

我认为很多技术水平较低的用户会喜欢桌面应用程序（或与托管提供商集成的网络应用程序），让他们通过 GUI 管理网站，使用分屏视图预览更改，然后上传到网络主机。类似于 Microsoft Frontpage 之类的东西。

我怀疑 Neocities 如此受欢迎的部分原因是它的网页编辑器，如果有一个更强大的版本那就太好了。如果它与 Git 集成以允许版本跟踪和多个作者协作，那就更好了。

> The truth is, there has been no successful CMS for static-site generators because the only people that give a fuck about creating static sites would much prefer to use a (free and local) IDE and a terminal.

I think a lot of less technical users would love a desktop app (or a web app integrated with their hosting provider) that lets them manage their website via a GUI, preview their changes with a split-screen view, and upload to a web host. Something similar to Microsoft Frontpage or the like.

I suspect the reason that Neocities got so popular was in part because of its web editor, and having a more powerful version of that would be fantastic. If this integrated with Git to allow version tracking and multiple authors to collaborate, even better.

在 HN 中查看
MidnightRider39 2026/04/12 11:22
我的个人页面自过去 3 年以来一直在 11ty 上运行，我非常喜欢它。

我可能很快就会用纯 HTML 替换它 - 我发现我不再需要 SSG，我可以使用本地 LLM 从 Markdown 文件生成 HTML，而且我从来不使用任何花哨的功能。

My personal page runs on 11ty since the last 3 years and I enjoyed it a lot.

I’ll probably replace it with pure HTML soon - I found that I don’t need a SSG anymore, I can just use a local LLM to generate HTML out of markdown files and I never use any fancy features anyway.

在 HN 中查看
npilk 2026/04/12 12:09
是的，我发现法学硕士非常适合将结构松散的文本转化为格式化的博客文章。 https://notes.npilk.com/chatgpt-is-my-ssg
Yes, I find LLMs are great for taking loosely-structured text and turning them into formatted blog posts. https://notes.npilk.com/chatgpt-is-my-ssg

schaefer 2026/04/12 13:09

I'm sure you know about pandoc for translating markdown into html (and all it's other tricks).

bryanhogan 2026/04/12 12:40
Astro 在这里工作不是很好吗？

只是一个没有 JavaScript 的静态网站，但您仍然可以获得一些不错的东西，例如作用域 CSS、组件以及能够在博客文章中使用 Markdown。

Wouldn't Astro work great here though?

Just a static sites without JavaScript but you still get some nice things like scoped CSS, components and being able to use markdown for blog posts.

在 HN 中查看
elevation 2026/04/12 11:43
我还在几个项目中使用 11ty，但我讨厌 npm 生态系统。

我正在考虑让 LLM 生成一个平面 python 脚本来代替 11ty 为我做的事情。 一旦脱离喧嚣，它应该会稳定数十年。

I'm also using 11ty on a couple projects, but I abhore the npm ecosystem.

I'm considering letting an LLM generate a flat python script to replace what 11ty does for me. Once removed from the fracas, it should be stable for decades.

deadbabe 2026/04/12 12:14
如果使用 LLM 为什么要费心使用 python？选择直接的 shell 脚本。
If using an LLM why bother with python? Go for straight shell scripts.

gwerbret 2026/04/12 12:36
如果有人想知道的话，标题是艾萨克·阿西莫夫的书《永恒的终结》的一个剧本： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_End_of_Eternity
In case anyone wondered, the title is a play on the Isaac Asimov book "The End of Eternity": https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_End_of_Eternity

replygirl 2026/04/12 13:47
这显然是指新世纪福音战士的结尾
it's clearly a reference to the end of evangelion

nchmy 2026/04/12 12:55

在 HN 中查看
OuterVale 2026/04/12 11:12
我仍然完全爱上了 WebOrigami（https://weborigami.org）。它是一种“JavaScript 方言”，专为构建静态站点而设计。它不是很受欢迎，但它比我发现的其他任何东西都更加灵活和全面。很好地填补了110的空白。
I'm still completely in love with WebOrigami (https://weborigami.org). It is a 'dialect of JavaScript' that is designed for building static sites. It isn't super popular, but it much more flexible and comprehensive than anything else I've found. Fills the 11ty gap nicely.
在 HN 中查看
morpheuskafka 2026/04/12 11:02
> 谁使用 11ty？ NASA、CERN、TC39 委员会、W3C、Google、Microsoft、Mozilla、Apache、freeCodeCamp 等等。

> 想象一下，如果 Build Awesome 真的接触到经常制作静态网站的人。您知道 NeoCities、MelonLand 或 32 位 Cafe 上的用户群吗？

前一分钟您还在说大公司使用该产品，下一分钟您就说它始终是为业余爱好者提供的，不应该针对企业功能？

> 事实上，我自己也创办了一家与 Build Awesome 有着几乎相同理念的企业。 Berry House 是我的独立网络工作室

> 不同的是，我的模式是按需付费或无偿付费。我为客户开发了 Calgary Groups，并为我的开发工作收取 5 美元/小时的费用。

那不是生意——没有利润动机。 （甚至工作工资低于最低工资。）这不是一个比较像 Font Awesome 这样的实际企业应该做什么的好基准。

> Who uses 11ty? NASA, CERN, the TC39 committee, W3C, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, Apache, freeCodeCamp, to name a few.

> Imagine if Build Awesome actually reached out to people who regularly make static sites. You know, the userbases on NeoCities or MelonLand or 32-bit Cafe?

One minute you are saying large companies use the product, the next that it was always for hobbyists and shouldn't target corporate features?

> In truth, I myself have started a business that has a near identical concept to Build Awesome. Berry House is my independent web studio

> The difference is though that my model is pay-what-you-can, or pro bono. I developed Calgary Groups for a client and charged $5/hour for my dev work.

That is not a business -- no profit motive. (Working less than minimum wage, even.) Not a good benchmark for comparing what an actual business like Font Awesome should do.

在 HN 中查看
fsckboy 2026/04/12 10:48
如果 Kickstarter 活动达到了目标，但他们发出的电子邮件最终进入了垃圾邮件文件夹，为什么会说取消？他们引用了“势头”，但筹款的成功难道没有维持项目和团队的势头吗？解决了邮件问题，再给赞助商发邮件，有什么大不了的，既然目标已经达到了，赞助商什么时候还需要动力呢？
if the kickstarter campaign met its goals, but then their outgoing emails ended up in the spam folders, why does that say cancel? They cite "momentum", but doesn't the fundraising success sustain the momentum of the project and team? solve the email problem and mail the sponsors again, what's the big deal, since when do sponsors need momentum if the goal has been met?
在 HN 中查看
ChrisArchitect 2026/04/12 11:04
这。完全令人困惑。听起来像是一次非常成功的活动，达到了目标。为什么该博客文章的其余部分（https://blog.fontawesome.com/pausing-kickstarter/）如此消极，就像是一个很大的失望？就像 Font Awesome 期望从中获得一些长期不断增长的收入来源一样？太奇怪了。 （而且，首先我看到了与此相关的任何内容，或者有任何理由关注 Font Awesome 正在做什么，尽管我是 11ty 的常规用户并参与其小型生态系统多年）

如果“110 开发者”不是变革的忠实粉丝，那么谁会急于支持 Kickstarter？谁资助这个项目（为什么？）

This. Totally confusing. Sounded like a very successful campaign, met goal. Why is the rest of that blog post (https://blog.fontawesome.com/pausing-kickstarter/) so negative and like it's a big disappointment? Like Font Awesome was expecting some lengthy constantly growing source of income from it? So weird. (and also, first I'm seeing anything about this or given any reason to pay attention to what Font Awesome is doing despite being a regular user of 11ty and involved with its small ecosystem for years)

And if '11ty devs' aren't big fans of the change etc, then who was rushing to support the Kickstarter? Who's funding this (and why?)

在 HN 中查看
ChrisArchitect 2026/04/12 11:11
撇开但相关：希望上个月它坏掉时就在这里分享。

https://www.11ty.dev/blog/build-awesome/

可以肯定地说，HN 左右有一些人已经使用/正在使用 11ty，并且可能有一些兴趣。

我感谢 Zach 多年来的奉献精神，并相信 11ty 所代表的含义（以及最近 webc 为桌面/生态系统带来的内容）

Aside, but related: wish this had been shared around here last month when it broke.

https://www.11ty.dev/blog/build-awesome/

Safe to say there's a number around HN who have used/are using 11ty and might have some interest.

Am grateful for Zach's dedication over the years and believer in what 11ty stands for (and more recently what webc brings to the table/ecosystem)

在 HN 中查看
busterarm 2026/04/12 12:30
作者似乎认为这些工具只有一种类型的用户。 即使用 NeoCities 的人。 他们对他来说都很重要。

早在 2015 年，我就使用 Jekyll 和 Wordpress CMS 作为后端构建一个大型静态网站。 我们有 30 多个内容编辑器使用它来编写 Markdown，我还有 Jekyll Generator，它可以对 Wordpress 数据库执行 SQL 查询来构建静态内容。 每个新帖子都会在 2-3 分钟内构建和部署整个网站。 超过 50k 页的内容。 这为一个非常大的营销网站提供了支持，该网站横跨多个排名前 10 的 Google 广告关键字。 业务带来了数亿美元的 ARR。

据我所知，至少还有另外 3 个类似的大型企业也在这样做。 追溯到 2015 年。我现在的公司今天从 Wordpress 后端构建了一个静态网站，而我完全没有参与这项工作（甚至没有建议）。 这位笔者认为是传说的用户是非常非常真实的。 SSG 的 CMS 后端绝对有市场。

并不是说我作为一名开发人员会使用它，但如果我的雇主可以向公司支付我在一个漂亮盒子中构建的东西而不是支付我的工资，他们就会这样做。

The author seems to think that there's only one type of user of these tools. Namely people who use NeoCities. They're all that matter to him.

Way back in 2015 I was building a large static site using Jekyll and Wordpress CMS as a backend. We had 30+ content editors using it, writing Markdown and I had Jekyll Generator that would execute a SQL query against the Wordpress database to build static content. Every new post would build and deploy the whole site in 2-3 minutes. Over 50k pages of content. This powered a very large marketing website straddling multiple top 10 Google Ad keywords. Business was bringing in several hundred million in ARR.

I knew of at least 3 other similarly large businesses doing this. All the way back in 2015. My current company today builds a static site from a Wordpress backend and I was totally uninvolved in this work (or even suggesting it). The user that this author thinks is a fable is very very real. There is absolutely a market for a CMS backend for an SSG.

Not that I as a developer would have used it, but if my employers could have paid a company for what I built in a nice box instead of paying my salary, they would have.

在 HN 中查看
spankalee 2026/04/12 10:26
我几乎所有的静态站点都使用 Eleventy。我的几乎每个项目都至少有 110 个内部文档站点。我很高兴 Eleventy 有一个家，Zach 有一份工作。

但我对此唯一的想法是：十一是一个很棒的名字。

I use Eleventy for nearly all of my static sites. Almost every project of mine has at least an 11ty internal docs site. I'm very happy that Eleventy has a home and Zach a job.

But my only thought on this is: Eleventy is an awesome name.

在 HN 中查看
ETH_start 2026/04/12 12:54
具有讽刺意味的是，SDNY 对 Roman Storm 开发 Tornado Cash 的指控的一个关键点是他提供了一个 UI（因为后端智能合约已经作为法律问题确立为不可变的且不受 Roman Storm 的控制），而 Roman Storm 提供的 UI 是一个（开源）静态 HTML 文件，用户在自己的浏览器中执行客户端。
The irony is that a key plank of the SDNY's allegations against Roman Storm for his development of Tornado Cash is that he provided a UI (since the backend smart contract is already established as a matter of law to be immutable and outside of Roman Storm's control), and the UI that Roman Storm provided was an (open source) static HTML file that users executed client-side in their own browser.
在 HN 中查看
SoftTalker 2026/04/12 10:23
由于鼠标指针乱七八糟，所以没有阅读。为什么网站必须做这些废话。
Didn't read because of the fuckery with the mouse pointer. Why do sites have to do this crap.
在 HN 中查看
Brajeshwar 2026/04/12 10:50
已经有一段时间了（我想自从 Safari 引入阅读器模式以来），我几乎在阅读器模式下在网站上进行了所有阅读。对于某些网站，我设置为“可用时使用阅读器模式”，例如 paulgraham.com、daringfireball.net 以及其他一些排版很糟糕的网站。
It has been a while (I think ever since Safari introduced Reader Mode), and I do almost all my reading on websites in Reader Mode. For some websites, I have set to “Use Reader Mode when Available,” such as that of paulgraham.com, daringfireball.net, and quite a few others with horrible Typography.
在 HN 中查看
Dylan16807 2026/04/12 12:04
您是否认为自定义鼠标图标是垃圾/操蛋，让您离开网站，或者我错过了什么？ 如果你是这样的话，那就是一个相当大的过度反应。 并且可能值得您通过 userContent.css 或样式扩展设置单行以在所有站点上禁用 `cursor` 。
Are you calling a custom mouse icon crap/fuckery that makes you leave the site, or was there something I missed? If you are that's a pretty big overreaction. And it's probably worth you setting the single line to disable `cursor` on all sites via userContent.css or a styling extension.
在 HN 中查看
Sajarin 2026/04/12 12:53
您可以将其关闭，页面底部有一个小开关。
You can turn it off, there's a little toggle at the bottom of the page.
在 HN 中查看
